rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Respect AADL scope rules: component type contracts govern ports, implementation contracts govern subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE annexes in valid scopes without redesigning component structure.
Use only identifiers and types present in the analyzed AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
Ensure AGREE expressions align with AADL data types and port directions to prevent type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed in appropriate AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Avoid using event ports as numeric or Boolean values; only use explicit data or event-data values from the model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
